extraction.one_line_summary: HINDLEY Jai secured first Australian Giro victory by maintaining 1:25 GC lead over CARAPAZ Richard in final 17.4km Verona TT, aided by KÄMNA Lennard's Stage 20 work.
tactical_patterns: KÄMNA Lennard's domestique effort on Stage 20 Fedaia pre-positioned HINDLEY Jai's TT cushion, revealing multi-stage GC defense tactics.
HINDLEY matched CARAPAZ exactly through climb checkpoints (1 second difference), showing defensive pacing mastery under GC pressure.
ARENSMAN Thymen's Grand Tour debut produced 2nd place TT finish, 22.6 seconds down, signaling emerging stage race capability.

rider_signals.SOBRERO Matteo: positive: Won by 76 seconds in national champion skinsuit, second TT victory for team after YATES Stage 1.
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: Zero time gap to CARAPAZ at climb summit reveals controlled risk management, not raw TT power.
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: positive: 22.6 seconds behind winner on Grand Tour debut signals precocious TT ability for future GC campaigns.
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: Stage 20 mountain domestique work mentioned as critical factor in creating defendable 1:25 GC gap.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Matched HINDLEY's splits throughout but 1:25 Stage 20 loss proved insurmountable despite birthday effort.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Secured podium three years after missing it, showing late-career consistency improvement.
rider_signals.HEPBURN Michael: neutral: Set early benchmark but superseded, suggesting team time trial specialists struggle on mixed terrain parcours.
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: neutral: Led briefly before SCHMID, confirming puncheur TT ability fades on technical descents.
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: neutral: Local rider from 40km away couldn't match SOBRERO, showing home advantage limited in specialist TTs.
